question: When a brand comes to you worried about AI search visibility, what's the first wrong assumption you have to undo?
Have you had a client where doing the "right" AEO/GEO things made no visible difference — and what did that teach you?
What does good execution actually look like in this space, versus what most agencies pitch?
Everyone is talking about tools to track AI citations. What's the thing nobody is talking about that actually moves the needle?
The market has about 6 different names for this — AEO, GEO, LLMO, GSO. Does it matter? What do you call it and why?
Is AI search visibility actually measurable right now, or are brands buying confidence more than outcomes?
Which types of brands are genuinely winning in AI search right now — and what do they have in common that has nothing to do with their tech stack?
Reddit, LinkedIn, and YouTube are apparently among the most-cited sources by LLMs. What does that mean for how brands should think about content, and are your clients ready to hear it?
Where do you think this space is in 12 months — more consolidated, more chaotic, or something else entirely?
If you had to bet on one thing that will separate the brands that win AI visibility from those that don't — what is it, and why is it almost never what people focus on?
What do you do today, and what's the one thing you're proudest of about it?
What belief or approach separates how you work from most people in your field?
When did you make a call that went badly — and what did it force you to learn?
What's a moment when you were the underdog — and what happened?
Tell me about a person — mentor, rival, difficult boss, or early champion — who changed your trajectory. What did they do or say? What did you do with it?
When did you have to say something uncomfortable — to a boss, client, team, or room — and what did it cost or earn you?
What's something you believed early in your career that you now think is completely wrong?
When did you have to start over — new role, new industry, new city — and what surprised you most?
